```py app/egreen/lib/device/sensor/serializers.py
"""Serializers for sensor module."""

import json
from datetime import date, time, datetime
from rest_framework import serializers, status

import itertools

from django.db.models import Q

from egreen.lib.utils import compute_hash, get_or_none

from egreen.lib.device.actuator.models import Actuator
from egreen.lib.building.models import Building
from egreen.lib.building.reference.serializers import ReferenceWithoutSensorSerializer
from egreen.lib.authentication.exceptions import APIAuthenticationException
from .models import (
    DataType,
    Data,
    Sensor,
    SensorTag,
    SensorLevelGroup,
    SensorLevel,
    DataAnnotation,
)
from .exceptions import DataAPIException, SensorException
from .fields import SensorCodeField
from .business.models import DataComputed
from .utils import update_map_image


class DataTypeSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    """Serializer related to DataType model."""

    class Meta:
        model = DataType
        fields = ("id", "unit", "displayed_unit", "name", "name_cc", "is_average")


class DataAnnotationSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    created_by = serializers.SerializerMethodField()

    def get_created_by(self, obj):
        return obj.created_by.username if obj.created_by else None

    class Meta:
        model = DataAnnotation
        fields = [
            "id",
            "created_by",
            "created_by_id",
            "created_by_image",
            "message",
            "group_by",
            "created_at",
            "updated_at",
            "datetime",
            "sensor",
        ]


class GroupedAnnotationsField(serializers.Field):
    def to_representation(self, value):
        """
        `value` is expected to be a defaultdict(list) where the keys are the group_by values,
        and the values are lists of annotation dicts.
        """
        if not isinstance(value, dict):
            raise TypeError("Expected a dictionary for annotations")
        return {
            group: [dict(annotation) for annotation in annotations]
            for group, annotations in value.items()
        }


class DataComputedSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    """Serializer used to return formatted data."""

    money = serializers.DecimalField(
        max_digits=18, decimal_places=2, allow_null=True, source="price"
    )
    index = serializers.IntegerField(required=False, default=None, allow_null=True)
    value = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3)
    value_with_dju = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, default=None)

    value_with_dju_dt = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, default=None)

    dju = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=5, default=None)

    djc = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=5, default=None)

    djf = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=5, default=None)

    nb_occupant = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, default=None)
    open_hours = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, default=None)
    surface = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, default=None)
    occupation_rate = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, default=None)
    index = serializers.IntegerField(required=False)
    year = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=None, min_value=None, allow_null=True)
    month = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=12, min_value=1, allow_null=True)
    day = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=31, min_value=1, allow_null=True)
    hour = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=24, min_value=1, allow_null=True)
    minute = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=60, min_value=1, allow_null=True)
    second = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=60, min_value=1, allow_null=True)
    # weekday = serializers.IntegerField(max_value=7, min_value=1)
    co2 = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3)
    annotations = GroupedAnnotationsField(required=False)

    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        if "index" not in kwargs or not kwargs["index"]:
            self.fields.pop("index")
        kwargs.pop("index", None)
        super(DataComputedSerializer, self).__init__(*args, **kwargs)

    class Meta:
        model = DataComputed
        fields = (
            "money",
            "index",
            "value",
            "value_with_dju",
            "value_with_dju_dt",
            "dju",
            "djc",
            "djf",
            "nb_occupant",
            "open_hours",
            "surface",
            "occupation_rate",
            "year",
            "month",
            "day",
            "hour",
            "minute",
            "second",
            "co2",
            "annotations",
        )


class DataSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    """Serializer related to Data model."""

    sensor_id = serializers.SerializerMethodField()

    def get_sensor_id(self, obj):
        """Get sensor id associated to Data object."""
        return obj.sensor.id

    class Meta:
        model = Data
        fields = ("sensor_id", "rec_date", "rec_time", "index", "value")


class DataCreationSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    """
    Serializer used to create Data object.

    The difference with DataSerializer is about the sensor field. For the
    creation it the sensor code which is used and not the sensor id.
    """

    sensor_code = SensorCodeField(source="sensor", queryset=Sensor.objects.all())
    rec_date = serializers.DateField()
    rec_time = serializers.TimeField()
    index = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, required=False)
    value = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, required=False)

    def create(self, validated_data):
        """Override the default create method to check that hash is valid."""
        value = validated_data.get("value", None)
        index = validated_data.get("index", None)
        sensor = validated_data.get("sensor")
        rec_date = validated_data.get("rec_date")
        rec_time = validated_data.get("rec_time")
        request = self.context.get("request")

        if sensor.api_authentication != request.api_authentication:
            raise SensorException(
                code=SensorException.SENSOR_DOES_NOT_EXIST_CODE,
                message=SensorException.SENSOR_DOES_NOT_EXIST_MESSAGE,
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            )
        else:
            self.check_hash(request, validated_data)

        if value is None and index is None:
            raise DataAPIException(
                code=DataAPIException.MISSING_VALUE_OR_INDEX_CODE,
                message=DataAPIException.MISSING_VALUE_OR_INDEX_MESSAGE,
                status_code=status.HTTP_400_BAD_REQUEST,
            )

        # if value is None:
        #     validated_data['value'] = sensor.get_value_for_index(index)

        date = datetime.combine(rec_date, rec_time)
        return Data.create(sensor, date, index, value, sensor.factor)
        # return super().create(validated_data)

    def check_hash(self, request, data):
        """Check hash validity."""
        hash = request.META.get("HTTP_APIHASH", None)
        has_exception = False
        if hash is None:
            has_exception = True
        else:
            authentication = request.api_authentication
            rec_time = data.get("rec_time")
            rec_date = data.get("rec_date")
            sensor = data.get("sensor")
            computed_hash = compute_hash(
                sensor.code, rec_date, rec_time, authentication=authentication
            )

            has_exception = has_exception or computed_hash != hash

        if has_exception is True:
            raise APIAuthenticationException(
                code=APIAuthenticationException.HASH_INVALID_CODE,
                message=APIAuthenticationException.HASH_INVALID_MSG,
                status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            )

    # class Meta:
    #     model = Data
    #     fields = ('sensor_code', 'rec_date', 'rec_time', 'index', 'value')


class SensorTagSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    """Serializer related to the sensor tag model."""

    class Meta:
        model = SensorTag
        fields = ("id", "name", "is_average", "is_comparable")
        read_only_fields = ("id", "name", "is_average", "is_comparable")


class SensorLevelSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    id = serializers.IntegerField(required=False)

    class Meta:
        model = SensorLevel
        fields = (
            "id",
            "group",
            "lower_threshold",
            "higher_threshold",
            "lvl",
            "name",
            "color",
            "is_critical",
            "is_average",
        )


class SensorLevelGroupSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    sensor_levels = SensorLevelSerializer(many=True, required=False)
    building_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="building", queryset=Building.objects.all()
    )
    data_type_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="data_type", queryset=DataType.objects.all()
    )

    @staticmethod
    def get_overlap(a, b):
        return (a[1] - b[0] > 0) and (b[1] - a[0] > 0)

    def validate(self, attrs):
        """
        Check that sensor levels thresholds do not overlap.
        """
        attrs = super().validate(attrs)

        if "sensor_levels" not in attrs:
            return attrs

        sensor_levels = attrs["sensor_levels"]

        for a, b in itertools.combinations(sensor_levels, 2):
            overlap = self.get_overlap(
                [a["lower_threshold"], a["higher_threshold"]],
                [b["lower_threshold"], b["higher_threshold"]],
            )
            if overlap:
                raise serializers.ValidationError("Sensor levels thresholds do overlap.")

        return attrs

    def create(self, validated_data):
        levels = validated_data.pop("sensor_levels", [])
        group = SensorLevelGroup.objects.create(**validated_data)

        levels.sort(key=lambda level: level["lower_threshold"])
        lvl = 1
        for level in levels:
            SensorLevel.objects.create(group=group, lvl=lvl, **level)
            lvl += 1

        return group

    def update(self, group, validated_data):
        levels = validated_data.pop("sensor_levels", [])

        group_name = validated_data.pop("name", None)
        data_type = validated_data.pop("data_type", None)

        if group_name:
            group.name = group_name
            group.save()

        if data_type:
            group.data_type = data_type
            group.save()

        levels.sort(key=lambda level: level["lower_threshold"])
        lvl = 1
        for level in levels:
            if "id" not in level:
                SensorLevel.objects.create(group=group, **level)
                continue

            level_obj = get_or_none(SensorLevel, id=level["id"])

            if level_obj is None:
                return

            for key, value in level.items():
                setattr(level_obj, key, value)

            level_obj.lvl = lvl
            level_obj.save()

            lvl += 1

        return group

    class Meta:
        model = SensorLevelGroup
        fields = ("id", "building_id", "name", "sensor_levels", "data_type_id")


class SensorSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    """Serializer related to the sensor model."""

    building_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="building", queryset=Building.objects.all()
    )
    data_type_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="data_type", queryset=DataType.objects.all()
    )

    is_admin = serializers.SerializerMethodField("is_building_admin", read_only=True)

    reference_obj = serializers.SerializerMethodField()

    tags = SensorTagSerializer(many=True)

    level_group_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="level_group",
        queryset=SensorLevelGroup.objects.all(),
        allow_null=True,
    )

    thresholds = serializers.SerializerMethodField()

    # Get critical and average thresholds if they exist
    def get_thresholds(self, obj):
        # Return None if the object doesn't have an associated level group
        if not obj.level_group:
            return None

        # Initialize the structures for avg and critical thresholds
        thresholds = {"avg": None, "critical": None}

        # Iterate over each sensor level in the level group
        for sensor_level in obj.level_group.sensor_levels.all():
            # Use SensorLevelSerializer to serialize the sensor_level
            serialized_sensor_level = SensorLevelSerializer(sensor_level).data

            # Check if the sensor level is marked as average and set its threshold and color
            if sensor_level.is_average:
                thresholds["avg"] = {
                    "value": serialized_sensor_level["lower_threshold"],
                    "color": serialized_sensor_level["color"],
                }

            # Check if the sensor level is marked as critical and set its threshold and color
            if sensor_level.is_critical:
                thresholds["critical"] = {
                    "value": serialized_sensor_level["lower_threshold"],
                    "color": serialized_sensor_level["color"],
                }

        # Return the thresholds dictionary
        return thresholds

    def get_reference_obj(self, obj):
        from egreen.lib.building.reference.models import Reference

        try:
            serializers = ReferenceWithoutSensorSerializer(obj.reference)
            return serializers.data
        except Reference.DoesNotExist:
            return None

    def is_building_admin(self, obj):
        try:
            return True
            # user = self.context['request'].user
            # return obj.building in user.buildings_admin
        except KeyError:
            return False

    class Meta:
        model = Sensor
        fields = (
            "id",
            "building_id",
            "name",
            "code",
            "data_type_id",
            "is_manual",
            "is_global",
            "utility",
            "formula",
            "reference",
            "reference_obj",
            "ref_objective",
            "alert_instant_threshold",
            "alert_short_threshold",
            "alert_long_threshold",
            "is_admin",
            "tags",
            "first_data_date",
            "last_data_date",
            "is_virtual",
            "battery",
            "obj_threshold",
            "level_group_id",
            "thresholds",
            "usage",
        )
        read_only_fields = (
            "code",
            "data_type_id",
            "is_manual",
            "is_admin",
            "building_id",
            "reference",
            "reference_obj",
            "tags",
            "first_data_date",
            "is_virtual",
            "last_data_date",
        )

    def create(self, validated_data):
        """
        Override standard create method.

        It set the field is_manual to True for the created sensor. An automatic
        sensor is not created via the API (via backend).
        """
        sensor = super(SensorSerializer, self).create(validated_data)

        if not sensor.formula:
            sensor.is_manual = True
            sensor.save()

        return sensor


class ManualSensorDataCreationSerializer(serializers.Serializer):
    """
    Serializer used to create Data object.

    The difference with DataSerializer is about the sensor field. For the
    creation it the sensor code which is used and not the sensor id.
    """

    sensor_code = SensorCodeField(source="sensor", queryset=Sensor.objects.filter(is_manual=True))
    rec_date = serializers.DateField()
    rec_time = serializers.TimeField()
    index = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3)
    value = serializers.DecimalField(max_digits=18, decimal_places=3, required=False)

    def create(self, validated_data):
        value = validated_data.get("value", None)
        index = validated_data.get("index", None)
        sensor = validated_data.get("sensor")
        rec_date = validated_data.get("rec_date")
        rec_time = validated_data.get("rec_time")

        date = datetime.combine(rec_date, rec_time)
        return Data.create(sensor, date, index, value, sensor.factor)


class ManualSensorDataSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    """Serializer related to Data model."""

    sensor_id = serializers.IntegerField(source="sensorID")
    rec_time = serializers.SerializerMethodField()
    rec_date = serializers.SerializerMethodField()

    def get_rec_date(self, obj):
        return date(obj["year"], obj["month"], obj["day"])

    def get_rec_time(self, obj):
        return time(obj["hour"], obj["minute"], obj["second"])

    class Meta:
        model = Data
        fields = ("id", "sensor_id", "rec_date", "rec_time", "index", "value")


class SensorAlerteDeviceSerializer(serializers.ModelSerializer):
    building_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="building", queryset=Building.objects.all()
    )
    data_type_id = serializers.PrimaryKeyRelatedField(
        source="data_type", queryset=DataType.objects.all()
    )

    class Meta:
        model = Sensor
        fields = (
            "id",
            "name",
            "code",
            "data_type_id",
            "building_id",
        )
```

replace line 496 using the lib arrow
I'll help you update the code to use the `arrow` library for creating datetime objects.

<patch>
<title>Use arrow lib for datetime</title>

<edit>
<path>app/egreen/lib/device/sensor/serializers.py</path>
<description>Use arrow library to combine date and time instead of datetime.combine</description>
<operation>update</operation>
<old_text>date = datetime.combine(rec_date, rec_time)</old_text>  
<new_text>date = arrow.get(rec_date).replace(hour=rec_time.hour, minute=rec_time.minute, second=rec_time.second).datetime</new_text>
